# INTRO_CONTEXT.md — ARIS-Code Paper Introduction

**Generated by**: `intro-context` node in "Paper Introduction Flow E2E"  
**Date**: 2026-05-19  
**Status**: Paper context inventory — based on README, CHANGELOG, web/README, and v0.4.11 audit docs. No NARRATIVE_REPORT, PAPER_PLAN, or experiment results found in workspace.

---

## One-Sentence Contribution

ARIS-Code is a terminal-based adversarial multi-agent research CLI that drives the full academic research pipeline — from idea discovery and literature survey through paper drafting, review, and submission — via a natural-language executor-reviewer loop with 74 bundled skills.

---

## Target Audience & Venue Assumptions

| Dimension | Assumption |
|-----------|-----------|
| **Primary audience** | ML/AI researchers and PhD students seeking to automate routine research tasks |
| **Secondary audience** | Research lab leads evaluating automated research assistants |
| **Venue** | Not yet declared; defaults to **ICLR** (9 pages, main body to Conclusion) |
| **Paper type** | Systems/Methods paper with empirical evaluation — multi-agent orchestration, not theory-heavy |
| **Page budget** | 9 pages (ICLR default, excludes references + appendix) |
| **Anonymity** | Assume anonymized submission (no author names in draft) |

---

## Core Claims (3–5)

| # | Claim | Evidence Available | Status | Missing Evidence |
|---|-------|---------------------|--------|------------------|
| 1 | ARIS-Code enables end-to-end research automation from idea to submission via a natural-language CLI | README describes the full pipeline; 74 bundled skills cover idea-discovery → paper-write → paper-compile → auto-review-loop → rebuttal; CHANGELOG documents v0.1.0–v0.4.11 evolution | **Partial** | No controlled user study or task-completion rate measurement |
| 2 | The executor-reviewer adversarial architecture improves output quality through iterative critique | LlmReview tool description in README; v0.4.x changelog describes multi-round review loops; kill-argument two-thread adversarial review documented in audit | **Partial** | No ablation of reviewer vs. no-reviewer quality delta |
| 3 | ARIS-Code supports multiple LLM providers (Anthropic, OpenAI, Gemini, GLM, MiniMax, DeepSeek, Kimi, Qwen, Doubao) as executor and/or reviewer | README §Supported Providers table; CHANGELOG v0.4.3–v0.4.10 detail provider additions; multi-provider pricing registry in v0.4.10 | **Partial** | No benchmark of cross-provider accuracy/cost tradeoffs |
| 4 | The skill ecosystem (74 skills, 49 embedded helpers) is syntactically and semantically integrated with the CLI runtime through a canonical resolver chain | Integration-contract.md defines 6 failure policies; v0.4.11 audit documents canonical resolver rollout (closes research-wiki empty-week incident); SKILLS_SOURCE_COMMIT pinning mechanism | **Partial** | No evaluation of skill resolution success rate in production |
| 5 | ARIS-Code has been shipped and used through v0.4.11 with real users (GitHub releases, community contributors credited in CHANGELOG) | CHANGELOG lists 10+ credited community contributors (GitHub handles); release artifacts for macOS/Linux/Windows; v0.4.11 on 2026-05-18 | **Partial** | No usage statistics, retention numbers, or task success metrics |

---

## Missing Evidence (Critical Gaps for Introduction)

1. **No quantitative results** — the paper cannot make "method X outperforms Y by Z%" claims without experiments. Introduction must avoid overclaiming.
2. **No user study** — claims about productivity or quality improvement are unsubstantiated without user data.
3. **No ablation** — the value of the reviewer vs. executor-only cannot be quantified.
4. **No benchmark comparison** — claims about provider flexibility lack accuracy/speed/cost comparisons.
5. **No prior art analysis in workspace** — related work positioning needs literature search (use `/research-lit` or `/comm-lit-review`).
6. **No existing paper draft** — no LaTeX source, no figures, no tables.

---

## Citation & Literature Needs

The introduction will need to cite prior work in at least these areas (none in workspace — needs search):

1. **Multi-agent LLM systems** — e.g., works on LLM agents using tools, self-reflection, multi-agent debate (e.g., Voyager, Reflexion, Swarms, Multi-Agent Debate)
2. **Research automation tools** — e.g., Semantic Scholar, AutoGPT-style agents, Claude Code, other research assistants
3. **Adversarial review or critique systems** — foundational work on adversarial prompting, review loops, kill-argument patterns
4. **Skill/tool ecosystems for LLMs** — prior work on tool use, skill libraries, agent scaffolding (e.g., LangChain, AutoGen, ChatGPT Plugins)
5. **Software engineering automation** — relevant if framing ARIS as analogous to automated software research

### Suggested Immediate Actions Before Drafting Introduction

- Run `/research-lit` or `/comm-lit-review` to gather citations for related work
- Run `/novelty-check` to validate the research contribution angle
- Clarify the **single most important claim** the paper will make (one-sentence test from writing-principles.md)
- Decide on hero figure strategy — no experiment results means Figure 1 should be a system architecture diagram or workflow illustration

---

## Key Technical Details from v0.4.11 Audit (For Framing)

### Executor-Reviewer Architecture
- Executor LLM acts; Reviewer LLM critiques via `LlmReview` tool
- Iteration continues until quality converges
- Classic pairing: **Claude Executor + GPT/GLM Reviewer** for true adversarial research
- Supports **any provider pairing** — executor and reviewer can be from different providers

### Skill Ecosystem Scale
- **74 bundled skills** (v0.4.11), up from 42 in v0.1.0
- **49 embedded helpers** (tools/ + shared-references/)
- Canonical resolver chain: `.aris/tools/` → `tools/` → `$ARIS_REPO/tools/` → `$ARIS_CACHE_DIR/<version>/`
- 10 new skills in v0.4.11: citation-audit, experiment-queue, gemini-search, kill-argument, openalex, overleaf-sync, paper-talk, qzcli, resubmit-pipeline, slides-polish

### Web Console (ARIS Web)
- Single-user local web console for `aris-code` branch
- Browses bundled skills, launches `aris prompt` runs, streams logs
- DAG-based multi-agent orchestrator v1
- Node-level editing for prompt, skill, model, effort, dependencies
- Backend serves frontend at `/`; API at `/api/*`

### Reliability Engineering (v0.4.9–v0.4.10)
- Whole-stream restart on chunk abort / premature EOF (ARIS_STREAM_RETRY, default 2)
- MCP stdio reliability: 300s default timeout, dead-process detection, response.id ↔ request.id correlation
- Multi-provider pricing registry with accurate cache token accounting

---

## Working Assumptions for Introduction Draft

Based on available materials, the introduction will likely frame the paper around:

> **What**: ARIS-Code is a multi-agent CLI for automated research with an executor-reviewer adversarial loop and 74 bundled skills covering the full pipeline.  
> **Why it matters**: Researchers spend disproportionate time on routine tasks (literature search, paper writing, experiment planning); existing tools are single-agent and lack adversarial quality control.  
> **How**: Executor LLM writes/code/researches; independent Reviewer LLM critiques via LlmReview; iterations converge to higher-quality output.  
> **So what**: ARIS-Code is the most comprehensive open-source research automation CLI, covering the full pipeline and supporting multiple LLM providers with proven reliability (v0.4.11, 2026-05-18).

**WARNING**: The "so what" needs strengthening with evidence that does not yet exist in the workspace. The introduction must be careful not to overclaim.

---

## Next Steps (per paper-plan skill workflow)

1. **Create NARRATIVE_REPORT.md** — synthesize the story from README + CHANGELOG + web README + v0.4.11 audit docs; explicitly list claims and evidence gaps
2. **Run `/result-to-claim`** if any experiment results appear
3. **Clarify venue** — ICLR is the default but the team should decide (NeurIPS/ICML also supported)
4. **Clarify the hero claim** — one sentence that passes the "one-sentence contribution test"
5. **Gather citations** — run `/research-lit` or `/comm-lit-review`
6. **Decide on hero figure** — system architecture diagram (no experiment results available)
7. **Decide on anonymization** — ICLR vs IEEE affects author block and references
